reviews.review_msg: An amazing prof, incredibly caring and a fair marker. David does an incredible job catering to each student's needs and makes himself available for non-course related things as well. I asked David to be an academic reference for me and he was more than happy to be one. The most caring prof, highly recommend.

One of the best instructors I have ever had! The man not only knows how to teach but is an expert in emotional intelligence; he certainly has a gift for communication. Besides being honoured to have studied under the supervision of someone with his abilities, David has helped inspire me to publish my work and I am forever grateful. Recommended!!

This class was actually great to take remotely. David encouraged discussion during class, provided valuable insights, selected fascinating books, and provided awesome feedback on papers. Very kind and made sure to check in with students and be understanding of difficulites due to Covid. Please take a class with David, you won't regret it!

I honestly wish I had more time at SFU just so I could have more classes with David. Easily the best professor I've had in my whole undergrad. Very caring and considerate of his students. He's also super approachable if you need help, feedback, or just want to chat. Made online learning during COVID actually enjoyable and non-stressful.

David is super knowledgeable and one of the kindest professors Ive met at SFU. He emailed each of us in the middle of the term just to check in and see how were doing. The feedback he gave on our assignments are also really helpful and super detailed. He made IS451 as enjoyable as it can be and made the most out of our zoom sessions!

Professor Cooper is a fantastic professor! She has a way of drawing out very interesting points from class discussion. The seminars were always insightful and thought-provoking. The course involved a lot of readings and papers, but the workload was manageable. Overall, great course.

Professor Cooper is one of the best professors I've had at SFU. Not only does she have extensive experience and is brilliant, but she's easygoing and accessible. She assigns great, relevant books and focuses on theories as well as their practical applications. I'd definitely recommend Professor Cooper!

Material from interdisciplinary fields that we may have NOT been familiar with, but he expects you to understand it like if we been engaged with it all our lives. Very HARSH marker. He expects you to write word for word &criticizes ALL other readings, but his. He feels he is superior than others. Knowledgeable- But, a BAD teacher. Classes horrible

Harriss is the best prof I have had at SFU. He is extremely knowledgable and sharp, but still very kind and approachable. I learned more in his once class than I think I have in the rest of my first two years combined.

Professor Harriss is a great professor. His lectures are well structured and helped a lot for the papers. While he is somewhat quirky, it is clear that he really cares about his students. That being said, you will have to work very hard in his class. This is the most intense class I've taken but it was worth it.

He's a nice guy, very friendly and knowledgeable. A slow talker. He also isn't very helpful. He doesnt give criteria (because you shouldn't need instructions in 4th year) so I was trying to make sure I knew what to do, and he acted offended that I even asked. He barely talked about the assignment as if it wasn't even for marks.
He is extremely arrogant.  He obviously knows a lot but some people just are not born to teach.  He should stick to his research.

I've had Harriss for 101, 450 and 451. He's an articulate communicator and has extensive knowledge. He's friendly, and very helpful one on one. Lectures can be dry, but if you focus you get a lot out of them. I've never worked harder for a professor, but I've never learned so much either. If you can't put in the work, don't take IS.

Although a challenging class, it is well worth the effort. Harriss is not head of the department for nothing. He is a wealth of knowledge and when utilized properly can teach so much. Don't blame him for your laziness.
Maybe instead of writing upwards of 11 fake reviews he should consider the criticism he receives and seek to reform himself as a lecturer and person.

POL 210 (Intro Political Philosophy).  His focus on "justice" made for a totally absorbing journey.  Backpack spilling over with readings, which the prof unpacked superbly.  Passionate, always willing to explain.  He showed amazing patience for some *really* stupid questions; wish SFU had standards for who gets in.

If I hadn't overloaded my semester, this capstone class would likely have been even more rewarding -- and I'd probably give the prof a full 5. He was incredibly invested in the "core texts," and made me a fan of Canada's own Charles Taylor (whom I'd never read before).  Milestone: the "social imaginary" as a key way to understand the world.

POL 210.  Who thought political philosophy could be this much fun?  Right to make it mandatory.  Amazing prof who was committed to making *ideas* about justice as interesting and accessible as the usual political back-and-forth.  Put up with the usual slackers who are entitled and won't stop whining.

POL 421W - Equality and the Charter.  Totally engaged with students on how real world cases match up with theory.  Learned more in 12 wks than I have in a whole year of courses.  BTW: if you are one of those cruisers who want a lightweight summer course, look elsewhere (it's a "W" class, ok?) instead of whining on this site.

A memorable course (Core Texts in International Studies) to cap my major and graduate with a cheer.  Prof is super-literate and ridiculously well-informed on global affairs ... ask him anything.  The class was totally engaged in every session, a rarity in my 4 yrs here.

Sajoo is hands down the toughest professor I ever had in my four years at SFU. His assigned readings are very dense, hard and dry but it is necessary in order to understand the lecture. He can be a little degrading with his words. His assignments do not have a clear criteria. His lectures are fast is interesting.
the worst prof ever ... lectures are dry and the questions are extremely difficult
